page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-2 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a base speed of 2.3 GHz and includes 48 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 260 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 7.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.5 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The server demonstrates strong single-threaded capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2081.0 and high single-threaded compression throughput, reaching 34.12 GB/sec in gzip and 14.36 GB/sec in bzip3. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance is relatively weak. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, and the Passmark memory score is similarly positioned in the bottom 10%, with uncached large-block read bandwidth limited to 15.64 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-2 offers dedicated Xeon execution resources at a lower-tier resource density, making it suitable for workloads that require predictable single-threaded CPU performance without massive memory or multi-core demands. Tradeoffs include constrained memory bandwidth and limited multi-threaded scaling, as evidenced by lower scores in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. This server is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, simple web serving, and low-intensity background tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-2 is an x86_64 server featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 7.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2081.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds. However, the instance exhibits notable limitations in multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark memory metrics falling into the bottom 10% of tested systems. This hardware profile offers a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation but modest resource footprints. It is best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight web serving rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs and 7 GB RAM. It features strong single-core performance but exhibits limited multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-2 is an x86_64 server with 2 vCPUs and 7 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling.
tagline: A dedicated dual-vCPU x86_64 compute instance delivering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight, non-parallelized workloads and basic text processing.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 2 vCPUs and 7.0 GB RAM
Strong single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2081.0
Constrained multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth under heavy loads
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and light web serving
